title: Subword Tokenization — BPE, WordPiece, Unigram, SentencePiece

questions.question: What does subword tokenization buy you over word-level vocabularies?
options: Rare words decompose into known subword pieces, eliminating OOV while keeping vocabulary bounded
questions.explanation: Subword tokens cover any input by decomposition, removing the OOV problem of word-level vocab.

questions.question: Why does GPT-2 use byte-level BPE rather than character-level BPE?
options: A 256-byte base vocabulary covers any UTF-8 input, guaranteeing no [UNK] tokens
questions.explanation: Byte-level BPE starts from 256 bytes so every input encodes; nothing is OOV.

questions.question: How does the Unigram tokenizer build its vocabulary?
options: Start from a large candidate set, iteratively prune tokens whose removal least hurts corpus log-likelihood
questions.explanation: Unigram fits a unigram LM and iteratively removes the least useful tokens to reach target vocab size.

questions.question: What distinguishes WordPiece's merge criterion from BPE's?
options: WordPiece merges pairs that maximize training-corpus likelihood, while BPE merges the most frequent pair
questions.explanation: WordPiece picks merges by likelihood; BPE picks by raw frequency.

questions.question: Which tool trains a tokenizer directly on raw multilingual Unicode text?
options: SentencePiece (encodes whitespace as a special marker and trains BPE or Unigram)
questions.explanation: SentencePiece trains BPE/Unigram on raw text without pre-tokenization; tiktoken only encodes.

questions.question: Why must production CI hash-check the deployed tokenizer.json?
options: Tokenizer drift produces different token IDs from those the model was trained on, silently corrupting outputs
questions.explanation: Even small tokenizer changes shift IDs; a hash check catches drift before it reaches users.

questions.question: What is a common reason a single emoji takes many tokens?
options: Multi-codepoint emojis encode into multiple UTF-8 bytes; without dedicated tokens each byte is its own subword
questions.explanation: Composite emojis encode as several bytes; byte-level tokenizers may use multiple tokens per glyph.

questions.question: What heuristic guides vocabulary size for a new monolingual transformer?
options: Roughly 32k for models under 1B parameters; 50-100k for 1-10B; 200k+ for multilingual or frontier models
questions.explanation: Vocab size scales with model and language coverage; these are rough community defaults.
